name: Bear Dance Driving Range
about: The Golf Club at Bear Dance is a championship golf facility set within 753 acres of mountainous terrain, featuring an 18-hole course with tree-lined fairways, water features, and greens. The venue is located at an average elevation of 6,800 feet, providing panoramic views of the Front Range and Rocky Mountains. It operates as a public course consistently rated as the best and most challenging in Colorado along the Front Range, with options for tee time bookings, memberships, and various golf-related services.
why_golfers_like_it: Championship-level mountain golf experience with stunning views
Consistently rated as Colorado's best and most challenging public course
Variety of membership options to suit different needs
PGA instruction and professional services available
Host to corporate outings and private events

food_and_drink.notes: Restaurant likely serves alcohol, though not explicitly stated

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The venue offers club rentals through its pro shop, though players are welcome to bring their own equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: On-site dining is available through the restaurant, but outside food and drink policies are not specified.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The course is consistently rated as challenging, but specific pace of play times are not provided on the website.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The venue hosts family-oriented events and memberships, but specific kid-friendly amenities or junior programs are not detailed.
